phase1.output: (Window with interactive controls that update the label when events occur)
phase1.explanation:
Event handling makes your UI interactive:

Button events:
• setOnAction(e -> { ... })
• Lambda syntax: (event) -> { code }
• Also works with TextField, ComboBox, etc.

Property Listeners:
• addListener((obs, oldVal, newVal) -> { ... })
• Used for: valueProperty, selectedItemProperty, etc.

Common events:
• Button: setOnAction()
• TextField: setOnAction() (Enter key), setOnKeyPressed()
• CheckBox: setOnAction()
• ComboBox: setOnAction()
• ListView: getSelectionModel().selectedItemProperty()
• Slider: valueProperty()

Key event types:
• setOnKeyPressed() — key press
• setOnKeyReleased() — key release
• setOnMouseEntered() — mouse enters
• setOnMouseClicked() — mouse click

Lambda syntax:
• btn.setOnAction(e -> System.out.println("Clicked"))
• btn.setOnAction(e -> { multiple lines; })

Listeners:
• ObservableValue + ChangeListener
• (observable, oldValue, newValue) -> { ... }

Always use events to make your UI dynamic!

phase2.errorMessage: (Button appears but does NOTHING when clicked — no error, just silent failure!)

phase3.prompt:
Write a JavaFX program with:
1. A TextField for user input
2. A Button "Submit"
3. A Label that displays the text entered

When the button is clicked, the label should update with "Hello, [text]!"
When the user presses Enter in the TextField, the label should also update.
dialogueHints: TextField also has setOnAction() for Enter key
Use setText() to update the label
phase3.openingDialogue: Events make your UI come alive! ⚡
